name: Aura Clash launch evidence manifest
purpose: Defines the evidence files and commands required before remaining Aura Clash showcase launch checkboxes may be marked complete.

requiredProof: `vercel-deploy.json` contains a production deployment URL and at least one Vercel deployment URL.

465.checkbox: Confirm deployed route and GLB URLs return 200.
requiredProof: All canonical Aura Clash route probes return HTTP 200.
Every manifest-referenced GLB probe returns HTTP 200 with a nonzero byte length or content length.
The deployed `/showcase/aura-clash/` route family, sitemap, robots, and manifest-referenced GLBs pass the route evidence probe.

proves: Every public launch GLB has provenance, license note, thumbnail, bounds, material summary, and intended route usage.
Every playable fighter uses a typed GLB with no approved primitive fallback.
Playable fighter source validation evidence includes scale, pivot policy, facing policy, bounds, material readability, and thumbnail coverage.
notes: launch-evidence.command-plan.json lists the durable command-proof outputs required before the cross-runtime evidence checker can close command gates.
The PRD updater uses this file only for source-quality typed-GLB/provenance coverage.
This is source-only PRD evidence and does not replace browser screenshot, review-package, or human visual approval gates.

proves: collectGameRuntimeEvidence reports loop, runtime nodes, input, kinematic bodies, collision, animation, effects, camera, assets, stage, HUD, accessibility, and subsystem ownership.
The showcase exports route-source runtime evidence and displays hitbox, animation, and physics body fields from the live runtime.
Runtime effect instances are pooled and can follow configured Aura3D/gameplay attachments through source-owned attachment position resolvers.
notes: This source evidence does not replace browser screenshots or route smoke output.
Ownership evidence is explicit; app-owned gameplay decisions are not mislabeled as renderer internals.

proves: Fighting stage source exposes foreground, combat-lane, midground, skyline, and parallax layers with nonblocking rules.
validateStage checks positive safe-zone area, safe-zone containment inside combat bounds, camera-safe layers, and no fighter-blocking layers.
The showcase stage scene mounts a typed Aura Clash duel-stage GLB plus fog, bloom, lights, and camera-safe source markers.
notes: This source evidence does not mark screenshot visual approval gates.

proves: Game runtime helpers are exported as named functions and typed namespaces without loading external GLB fixtures by default.
Fighting stage presets are procedural unless an app explicitly imports typed GLB assets.
Public examples use typed assets and public Aura3D imports instead of private renderer internals or string asset ids.
notes: Package-size and tree-shaking behavior still require package smoke/build evidence before release.

proves: Build app and marketing site.
Gameplay smoke passes.
`apps/aura-clash-showcase` asset gate output: `npm run assets:check` must pass and confirm only manifest-referenced GLBs are shipped.
`apps/aura-clash-showcase` route metadata output: `npm run routes:check` must pass and confirm canonical, Open Graph, Twitter, sitemap, and final `/showcase/aura-clash/` routes.
Aura Clash production build output: `npm run build` must complete successfully from `apps/aura-clash-showcase`.
Aura Clash browser smoke evidence: playable, route-health, deploy-check, and screenshot specs must pass against the built app.
Marketing production build output: `npm run build` must complete successfully from `marketing`.
`apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/local-gates.json` reports `ok: true`.
First-frame screenshot artifact: screenshot capture must produce a nonblank image showing the current Aura Clash route.
Default screenshot wiring captures the arena-establishing first frame plus fighter-readability and effects/HUD/debug compositions unless route or environment declarations override them.
`apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/first-frame.json` reports `ok: true`.
notes: The updater requires screenshot metadata and the screenshot file before marking screenshot metadata gates.
The updater requires screenshot-smoke evidence or an explicit nonblank metadata field before marking the nonblank screenshot artifact gate.
The updater marks `Capture and review first-frame screenshot.` only when screenshot metadata exists and `review-package.md` exists.
The updater never marks visually reviewed or user-approved screenshot gates from screenshot capture alone.

proves: Deploy to Vercel.
Vercel production deployment evidence: deployment command must finish and return the production URL.
`apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/vercel-deploy.json` reports `ok: true`.
Confirm deployed route and GLB URLs return 200.
Deployed route confirmed.

Deployed route proof: `/showcase/aura-clash/`, `/showcase/aura-clash/playable/`, `/showcase/aura-clash/evidence/`, `/showcase/aura-clash/accessibility/`, `/showcase/aura-clash/deploy-check/`, and `/showcase/aura-clash/poster/` must return successful responses.

Deployed GLB proof: every manifest-referenced GLB under `/showcase/aura-clash/aura-assets/` or `/apps/aura-clash-showcase/aura-assets/` must return a successful response with a nonzero content length.
`apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/deployed-routes.json` reports `ok: true`.
Current 1.0.6 contextual launch artifacts exist and are generated from Aura Clash Arena Playwright output.
`aura-clash-106-readiness.json` reports `ok: true` and references current flagship gates plus deployed-compatible route proof.
PRD-named compatibility screenshots map to current contextual Aura Clash Arena screenshots, not stale attempt artifacts.
notes: This command does not replace human visual approval.
Use the full deployed route evidence command with AURA_CLASH_ORIGIN when public production status must be proven.

proves: Human review package exists for visual approval.
Capture and review first-frame screenshot.
Review package source wiring surfaces first-frame metadata, screenshot compositions, launch asset visual source evidence, and visual approval artifact status without approving the gate automatically.
`apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/review-package.md` exists.
notes: The review package summarizes evidence for human review, but does not self-approve visual quality.
The updater still requires durable `launch-evidence/visual-approval.json` metadata before marking user visual approval or subjective visual-quality checkboxes.

proves: Visual screenshot approved by user.
User visual approval: the screenshot must be explicitly approved by the user before the `Visual screenshot approved by user` checkbox is marked complete.
User explicitly approves the visual screenshot.
Approval evidence is bound to the reviewed screenshot, screenshot metadata, and review package by SHA-256 hash and byte size.
notes: Requires explicit user approval before execution.
Requires a named approver and confirmation through AURA_CLASH_APPROVED_BY plus AURA_CLASH_VISUAL_APPROVAL_CONFIRMED=1 or equivalent CLI flags.
The PRD updater rejects visual approval evidence unless screenshot, screenshot metadata, and review package hashes and byte sizes match the current files.
Does not prove deployment, local gates, package smoke, or AuraVoice sample approval.

proves: Aura3D `1.0.5` package smoke passes in a fresh external Vite app.
Run unit/browser/build validation for the new APIs and CLI commands.
Confirm generated `dist/engine/agent-api/index.d.ts` exposes the new public types.
`pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm build`, game-runtime command gates, and prompt-animation command gates.
Run `aura3d assets validate-cartoon` against an AuraVoice-backed cartoon sample.
AuraVoice sample episode renders from prompt to audio to Aura3D animation with synchronized captions and visemes.
AuraVoice sample episode render package includes video, thumbnail, captions, timeline, audio stems, and evidence JSON.
Character mouth movement stays within one frame at 30 fps of AuraVoice viseme timing.
Caption display stays within one frame at 30 fps of AuraVoice dialogue timing.
Browser screenshot gallery contains at least one title-safe thumbnail and three storyboard frames.
notes: This command does not generate package-smoke, browser, video, or AuraVoice proof artifacts.
It only aggregates existing durable reports and gives the PRD updater one evidence file to inspect.
The default validation-suite input is `tests/reports/aura3d104/validation-suite.json`.

Individual command-proof defaults include `tests/reports/aura3d104/typecheck.json`, `tests/reports/aura3d104/build.json`, `tests/reports/aura3d104/dist-agent-api-types.json`, `tests/reports/game-runtime/*.json`, and `tests/reports/prompt-animation/*.json`.
The default validate-cartoon input is `tests/reports/prompt-animation/validate-cartoon.json`.
Set AURA_CLASH_CROSS_RUNTIME_REQUIRED=1 when the command should fail on missing cross-runtime evidence.

proves: Every remaining unchecked Aura Clash showcase item is explicitly mapped to durable evidence or a manual approval artifact.
No evidence, deploy, screenshot, package-smoke, AuraVoice render, or subjective visual approval item is silently treated as source-complete.
Remaining unchecked items can be audited by artifact class before the PRD updater marks any checkbox complete.
notes: This command does not run builds, tests, browser captures, package smoke, AuraVoice renders, deploys, or approvals.
It only checks PRD coverage metadata and writes a coverage report.
A passing coverage report means the remaining work is classified; it does not mean the evidence artifacts exist.

proves: The launch command plan has required groups, ids, outputs, and no duplicate command ids.
The documented command-proof outputs are source-checked before expensive evidence execution.
notes: This source check does not execute the commands listed in the plan.

proves: The full launch command plan can be materialized into a durable dry-run report before execution.
Manual-only commands remain skipped unless forced or explicitly approved.
notes: The app-level script runs the command plan in dry-run mode unless `--execute` is explicitly passed to the underlying runner.

proves: Coverage-manifest required artifacts are declared by the command plan, launch manifest, source evidence, wildcard evidence class, or current source/static files.
AuraVoice, Aura3D package-smoke, screenshot, deployment, and approval artifact names cannot silently drift across the PRD coverage metadata and launch evidence plan.
notes: This is a source wiring check only; it does not run expensive commands or prove the artifacts exist unless they are static/source files.

proves: The nine remaining active PRD evidence gates are mapped to concrete artifact statuses and command paths.
Operators can see which launch evidence, deploy proof, screenshot proof, and approval artifacts are still missing before attempting PRD closure.
notes: This report is readiness classification only. It does not mark PRD checkboxes and does not replace generated evidence or user approval.

proves: No active Aura Clash showcase checkboxes remain unchecked before the thread goal is marked complete.
Any remaining open active checkbox is listed with line number, readiness gate id, missing artifact ids, and command path.
notes: This is a final audit gate. It fails while evidence/deploy/approval checkboxes remain open.
Set AURA_CLASH_PRD_UPDATE_DRY_RUN=1 for dry-run mode.
Run `npm run launch:approve-visual` only after explicit user approval to create durable visual-approval.json evidence.
Deployment checkboxes require vercel-deploy.json with ok:true; environment flags are not durable deployment evidence.
Aura3D package-smoke and AuraVoice prompt-animation checkboxes require cross-runtime-evidence.json with the corresponding artifact gates reporting true.
The updater only marks the `vercel-deploy.json` checkbox when that JSON file itself reports `ok: true`.

The updater only marks `Run `aura3d assets validate-game` against AuraClash production assets.` when local-gates evidence contains a passing `assets-validate-game`, `aura3d-assets-validate-game`, or `validate-game` result.

The updater only marks source-quality typed-GLB gates when `aura-clash-launch-asset-evidence.json` contains the required provenance, license, bounds, material, thumbnail, typed asset, and no-fallback fields.
Runs local gates, screenshot capture, and review-package generation by default.
Set AURA_CLASH_RUN_VERCEL_DEPLOY=1 only when production deployment is explicitly approved.
Set AURA_CLASH_RUN_DEPLOYED_EVIDENCE=1 after deployment.
Set AURA_CLASH_RUN_COMMAND_PLAN_CHECK=1 when the workflow should source-check launch-evidence.command-plan.json.
Set AURA_CLASH_RUN_COMMAND_PLAN=1 when the workflow should write command-plan-run.json in dry-run mode.
Set AURA_CLASH_RUN_EVIDENCE_WIRING_CHECK=1 when the workflow should source-check coverage-to-command-plan artifact wiring.
Set AURA_CLASH_RUN_READINESS_REPORT=1 when the workflow should write readiness.json/readiness.md with remaining PRD gate status.
Set AURA_CLASH_RUN_COMPLETION_AUDIT=1 when the workflow should fail unless active Aura Clash showcase checkboxes are all checked.
Set AURA_CLASH_RUN_CROSS_RUNTIME_EVIDENCE=1 after Aura3D package-smoke and AuraVoice prompt-animation proof artifacts have been generated.
Set AURA_CLASH_RUN_PRD_EVIDENCE_COVERAGE=1 when the workflow should write prd-evidence-coverage.json before the PRD updater runs.
Set AURA_CLASH_UPDATE_PRD=1 only when evidence should update docs/project/aura-clash-showcase.md.

proves: A single local-only command path exists for build/smoke/screenshot/review-package/source-wiring/readiness evidence without deploying.
The command does not set Vercel deploy, deployed-route evidence, PRD update, visual approval, cross-runtime aggregation, or final completion audit flags.
A single strict final command path exists for PRD update and completion audit after the required evidence artifacts, deployment proof, and visual approval have already been generated.
The completion audit fails while any active Aura Clash showcase checkbox remains unchecked.

requiredEvidence: apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/first-frame.json includes screenshotCount/compositionCount/captures metadata for the three visual-review compositions
local-gates `screenshot-smoke` passes or screenshot metadata includes an explicit nonblank field
canMark: First-frame screenshot artifact: screenshot capture must produce a nonblank image showing the current Aura Clash route.
`apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/first-frame.json` reports `ok: true`.
Capture and review first-frame screenshot.
`apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/review-package.md` exists.

requiredEvidence: apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/visual-approval.json with `ok: true`, `approved: true`, `approvedBy`, `approvedAt`, reviewed screenshot path, screenshot metadata path, review package path, and matching SHA-256 hashes plus byte sizes for all three files
canMark: Visual screenshot approved by user.
User visual approval: the screenshot must be explicitly approved by the user before the `Visual screenshot approved by user` checkbox is marked complete.
User explicitly approves the visual screenshot.
AuraClash screenshot package is explicitly approved by the user.
User visually approves the final AuraClash screenshot and the first AuraVoice animation sample if that sample is part of the same launch claim.
Capture and approve an AuraClash screenshot after the runtime route fixes.
`apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/first-frame.png` exists and is visually reviewed.

requiredEvidence: local-gates browser smoke evidence passes where relevant
canMark: Screenshot evidence shows debug overlays, readable fighters, effects, HUD, and stage depth.
Visual identity is memorable enough for marketing screenshots.
Lighting provides readable silhouettes and premium screenshot contrast.
AuraClash is not world-class until real runtime gameplay is implemented with responsive controls, animation states, camera direction, collision, effects, polish, and visual QA evidence.

requiredEvidence: first-frame metadata contains `compositionCount`/`screenshotCount` of at least 3 or an array of at least 3 screenshots, captures, or compositions
canMark: At least three screenshot compositions look good: match start, combat impact, super move/result.

requiredEvidence: For every launch GLB: provenance, license note, thumbnail, bounds, material summary, and route usage

For every playable fighter: deliveryMode `typed-glb`, typed asset key, scale, pivot policy, facing policy, bounds evidence, material readability evidence, thumbnail evidence, and fallbackApproval `none - clean typed GLB required`
canMark: Every launch GLB has provenance, license note, thumbnail, bounds, material summary, and intended route usage.
Each playable fighter has a clean typed GLB with no primitive fighter fallback in the launch route.

requiredEvidence: apps/aura-clash-showcase/launch-evidence/vercel-deploy.json with `ok: true` and a production deployment URL
canMark: AuraClash playable route passes local gates and deployed route/GLB proof.
Deploy and verify public route, sitemap, robots, and GLB URLs.
Existing Aura Clash launch gates pass with evidence.
All evidence artifacts are generated, attached, and reflected in the PRD checklist.
PRD updater marks only evidence-backed checkboxes.

staleArtifactPolicy: Manifest entries must not reference stale attempt-prefixed launch artifacts.
Manifest entries must not reference superseded playable-smoke screenshot names.

requiredEvidence: Explicit user approval in the conversation or release notes
remainingHumanGate.automationRule: The updater may only mark this checkbox when launch-evidence/visual-approval.json reports ok:true, approved:true, approvedBy, approvedAt, the expected gate, and references to the reviewed screenshot plus review package.
